summary: REQ-3 (client-side backup/download/restore) and REQ-4 (English in-repo personal-use docs) appear to be implemented. REQ-2 (backend persistence across upgrades) is not clearly implemented because the dataset/password are stored in non-stable `var` fields and no explicit preupgrade/postupgrade stable-memory handling is shown; persistence through upgrade cannot be confirmed from the diff.

missing_requirements.requirement: Persist the uploaded spare-parts dataset and maintenance password across backend canister upgrades so that rebuilding/redeploying the same canister does not erase previously uploaded Excel data (subject to the existing 7-day retention logic).
missing_requirements.reason: The backend stores `datasetRecord` and `maintenancePassword` as ordinary `var` fields, and the diff does not show any `stable` variables or `system preupgrade/postupgrade` hooks that would preserve these values across an upgrade. A migration function is referenced, but without stable state it is not sufficient to meet the persistence acceptance criteria.

hallucinated_features.description: Publish-to-Live helper UI, slug validation utilities, and a Live smoke-test flow.
hallucinated_features.reason: Not requested by REQ-2/REQ-3/REQ-4; these add new product features beyond dataset persistence, backup/restore, and documentation.

hallucinated_features.description: Global redesign/theme changes (warm sand/stone palette) and Tailwind theme token extensions.
hallucinated_features.reason: Not requested; requirements did not ask for visual redesign.

hallucinated_features.description: Search table enhancements (debounced filtering across nine fields, pagination to 100 results, responsive horizontal scroll).
hallucinated_features.reason: Not requested; requirements focus on persistence, backup/restore, and docs.
